title: Atomic witness+note insert in scan_tx is correct design: defend against simplification
description: scanner.rs scan_tx() calls insert_note_with_witness() which runs note INSERT + witness INSERT in a single SQLite transaction. This is the correct design: a note without a witness row is permanently excluded from spendable_notes() by the INNER JOIN, so the note is permanently unspendable if the witness insert fails separately. A contributor might suggest splitting this into two calls (insert_note then upsert_witness) for simplicity. Defend: the atomic insert is load-bearing for correctness. The orphaned-note scenario is not recoverable without a full rescan. Keep the combined insert.
